- `javascriptallonge-front-matter` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0001-0004` [temporal, quantitative] terms `harness, cache, c98ab3e62b35ab0f, images` statement `![](harness/cache/c98ab3e62b35ab0f/images/javascriptallonge.pdf-0002-04.jpg)`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0001-0006` [method, identity, attribute] terms `publishing, you, lean, act` statement `Lean Publishing is the act of publishing an in-progress ebook using lightweight tools and many iterations to get reader feedback, pivot until you have the right book and...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0001-0005` [relationship, procedure] terms `leanpub, empowers, authors, publishers` statement `Leanpub empowers authors and publishers with the Lean Publishing process.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.1-13` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, might, possible, suggest`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.1-13` | path `javascriptallonge-front-matter.md`
- `javascriptallonge-forewords-to-the-first-edition` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0002-0003` [method] terms `however, very, conservative, books` statement `However, I’m very conservative about books – let’s just say that if there was an aftershave scented to the essence of “Used Book Store” then I would be...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0002-0005` [unlabeled] terms `however, reg, sent, copy` statement `However, Reg sent me a copy of his book and I was humbled.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0002-0004` [uncertainty, relationship] terms `you, might, imagine, was` statement `So as you might imagine I was “skeptical” about the decision to release JavaScript Allongé as an ongoing ebook, with a pay-what-you-want model.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.14-29` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, might`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.14-29` | path `javascriptallonge-forewords-to-the-first-edition.md`
- `javascriptallonge-as-little-as-possible-about-functions-but-no-less` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0003-0002` [quantitative, comparison, identity] terms `javascript, functions, values, they` statement `In JavaScript, functions are values, but they are also much more than simple numbers, strings, or even complex data structures like trees or maps.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0003-0027` [quantitative, mechanism] terms `produces, value, just, case` statement `Just as 2 + 2 produces a value (in this case 4), applying a function to zero or more arguments produces a value as well.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0003-0005` [uncertainty, quantitative, relationship] terms `let, start, second, simplest` statement `Let’s start with the second simplest possible function.[16] In JavaScript, it looks like this:`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0003-0034` [method, identity] terms `you, used, sometimes, other` statement `> 17If you’re used to other programming languages, you’ve probably internalized the idea that sometimes parentheses are used to group operations in an expression like math, and sometimes...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0003-0016` [quantitative, requirement, attribute] terms `function, must, understand, whether` statement `But we must understand that whether we see [Function] or () => 0, internally JavaScript has a full and proper function.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.30-43` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, might, possible, suggest`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.30-43` | path `javascriptallonge-as-little-as-possible-about-functions-but-no-less.md`
- `javascriptallonge-closures-and-scope` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0004-0022` [identity] terms `functions, containing, one, more` statement `- Functions containing one or more free variables are called _closures_ .`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0004-0025` [identity] terms `here, some, pure, functions` statement `Here are some pure functions we’ve already seen:`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0004-0023` [identity] terms `pure, functions, easiest, understand` statement `Pure functions are easiest to understand.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.44-48` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.44-48` | path `javascriptallonge-closures-and-scope.md`
- `javascriptallonge-that-constant-coffee-craving` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0005-0044` [quantitative, attribute] terms `our, one, binding, environment` statement `We have one binding in the environment representing our regular argument, and another our “constant.” That’s more efficient, and it’s _almost_ what we wanted all along: A way...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0005-0046` [relationship] terms `const, learn, lot, more` statement `We’ll learn a lot more about const in future chapters, but here’s the most important thing we can do with const:`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0005-0047` [unlabeled] terms `const, keyword, introduces, one` statement `The const keyword introduces one or more bindings in the block that encloses it.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.49-61` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, possible`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.49-61` | path `javascriptallonge-that-constant-coffee-craving.md`
- `javascriptallonge-naming-functions` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0006-0034` [identity] terms `binding, name, not, examining` statement `In this book we are not examining JavaScript’s tooling such as debuggers baked into browsers, but we will note that when you are navigating call stacks in all...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0006-0022` [negative-evidence, function, relationship, attribute] terms `function, name, something, else` statement `This still does not _name_ a function, but as we noted above, functions written with the function keyword have an optional “something else.” Could that “something else” name...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0006-0018` [limitation] terms `str, always, block, cannot` statement `We always use a block, we cannot write function (str) str + str.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0006-0026` [uncertainty, identity] terms `name, function, confusingly, not` statement `Confusingly, the name of the function is not exactly the same thing as the name we may choose to bind to the value of the function.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0006-0007` [attribute] terms `function, javascript, does, syntax` statement `JavaScript _does_ have a syntax for naming a function, we use the function keyword.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.62-78` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, might`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.62-78` | path `javascriptallonge-naming-functions.md`
- `javascriptallonge-recipes-with-basic-functions` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0007-0004` [uncertainty, identity] terms `recipes, written, for, practicality` statement `The recipes are written for practicality, and their implementation may introduce JavaScript features that haven’t been discussed in the text to this point, such as methods and/or prototypes.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0007-0005` [uncertainty] terms `overall, recipe, will, fit` statement `The overall _use_ of each recipe will fit within the spirit of the language discussed so far, even if the implementations may not.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0007-0010` [quantitative, comparison, identity] terms `you, argument, either, want` statement `These two recipes are for quickly and simply applying a single argument, either the leftmost or rightmost.[48] If you want to bind more than one argument, or you...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0007-0001` [temporal, quantitative] terms `harness, cache, c98ab3e62b35ab0f, images` statement `![](harness/cache/c98ab3e62b35ab0f/images/javascriptallonge.pdf-0079-01.jpg)`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0007-0003` [unlabeled] terms `functions, having, looked, basic` statement `Having looked at basic pure functions and closures, we’re going to see some practical recipes that focus on the premise of functions that return functions.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.79-93` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, might`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.79-93` | path `javascriptallonge-recipes-with-basic-functions.md`

- `javascriptallonge-object-assign` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0014-0013` [provenance, identity] terms `assigning, properties, one, object` statement `Assigning properties from one object to another (also called “cloning” or “shallow copying”) is a basic building block that we will later use to implement more advanced paradigms...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0014-0011` [procedure] terms `discuss, prototypes, will, object` statement `And when we discuss prototypes, we will use Object.assign to turn this:`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0014-0008` [relationship] terms `you, extend, one, object` statement `You can extend one object with another:`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0014-0009` [quantitative] terms `const, inventory, shipment, apples` statement `**const** inventory = { apples: 12, oranges: 12 }; **const** shipment = { bananas: 54, pears: 24 } Object.assign(inventory, shipment)`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0014-0012` [quantitative] terms `queue, function, prototype, const` statement `**const** Queue = **function** () { **this** .array = []; **this** .head = 0; **this** .tail = -1 }; Queue.prototype.pushTail = **function** (value) { _// ..._ }; Queue.prototype.pullHead...` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.198-205` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `possible`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.198-205` | path `javascriptallonge-object-assign.md`

- `javascriptallonge-generating-iterables` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0016-0007` [relationship, identity] terms `iterator, whether, simple, functional` statement `Whether it’s a simple functional iterator, or an iterable object with a .next() method, an iterator is something we call repeatedly until it tells us that it’s done.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0016-0002` [temporal, quantitative] terms `harness, cache, c98ab3e62b35ab0f, images` statement `![](harness/cache/c98ab3e62b35ab0f/images/javascriptallonge.pdf-0224-03.jpg)`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0016-0004` [unlabeled] terms `iterables, look, cool, then` statement `Iterables look cool, but then again, everything looks amazing when you’re given cherry-picked examples.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.224-245` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `might, possible`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.224-245` | path `javascriptallonge-generating-iterables.md`
- `javascriptallonge-lazy-and-eager-collections` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0017-0008` [limitation, identity] terms `some, added, methods, only` statement `Some methods are only added to a few collections, some are added to all.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0017-0014` [provenance, requirement] terms `collection, fat, object, style` statement `This “fat object” style springs from a misunderstanding: When we say a collection should know how to perform a map over itself, we don’t need for the collection...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0017-0020` [relationship, identity] terms `here, lazycollection, mixin, any` statement `Here is LazyCollection, a mixin we can use with any ordered collection that is also an iterable:`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0017-0016` [requirement, attribute] terms `object-oriented, collections, should, definitely` statement `Object-oriented collections should definitely have methods for mapping, reducing, filtering, and finding.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0017-0005` [unlabeled] terms `how, itself, map, filter` statement `Each collection knew how to map itself (.map), how to fold itself (.reduce), how to filter itself (.filter) and how to find one element within itself (.find).` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.246-260` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `might, possible`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.246-260` | path `javascriptallonge-lazy-and-eager-collections.md`
- `javascriptallonge-interlude-the-carpenter-interviews-for-a-job` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0018-0007` [quantitative, function, relationship] terms `carpenter, arrived, early, for` statement `The Carpenter arrived early for his meeting with “Thing Software,” and was shown to conference room 13.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0018-0003` [provenance, mechanism] terms `time, carpenter, normally, worked` statement `The Carpenter normally worked through personal referrals, but from time to time a recruiter would slip through his screen.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0018-0014` [temporal, quantitative] terms `harness, cache, c98ab3e62b35ab0f, images` statement `![](harness/cache/c98ab3e62b35ab0f/images/javascriptallonge.pdf-0262-02.jpg)`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0018-0011` [relationship, attribute] terms `code, excuse, technical, conversation` statement `Many companies use white-boarding code as an excuse to have a technical conversation with a candidate, and The Carpenter felt that being asked to whiteboard code was an...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0018-0002` [unlabeled] terms `for, carpenter, was, javascript` statement `“The Carpenter” was a JavaScript programmer, well-known for a meticulous attention to detail and love for hand-crafted, exquisitely joined code.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.261-272` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, possible, unknown`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.261-272` | path `javascriptallonge-interlude-the-carpenter-interviews-for-a-job.md`
